confirmed.title: Source filter puts 'All' first and preselects it; user wants 'Recordings' default with 'All' last

confirmed.fix: Reorder the Picker tags at MacInboxView.swift:292-295 to Записи, Материалы, Wai, then Все last; in MacContentView.swift inboxInitialSourceKind add `case .inbox, .folder: return .recording` so the Inbox (and folder) panes open on Записи while 'Все' stays selectable.

MacInboxView.swift:292-295: the segmented Picker lists "All"/"Все" first with tag Optional<InboxSourceKind>.none, then Recordings/Записи, Materials/Материалы, Wai. MacContentView.swift:999-1009: inboxInitialSourceKind returns .recording/.item/.chat only for .allRecordings/.content/.wai; `default: return nil` (line 1007-1008) catches .inbox and .folder(_), and that nil flows into MacInboxViewModel (MacInboxView.swift:107 init, configureScope at 139/144-154) whose sourceKind drives the Picker binding (line 289) — so "All" is indeed first AND preselected for Inbox and folders. The "user wants Recordings default, All last" preference is not audit invention: it matches the user's own pending backlog task #2 ("Reorder library filter chips: 'Все' last, default = Записи"). The fix is sensible and safe: Picker selection is tag-based so reordering segments breaks nothing in RU or EN (labels share the same t() calls); `case .inbox, .folder: return .recording` is valid Swift and MacInboxView has exactly one call site (MacContentView.swift:895) reachable only from the five inbox-rendering sections, so no other caller is affected; the .recording default already works for the .allRecordings section today including RU/EN empty-state copy ("Записей пока нет"); "Все" remains selectable via the nil tag. One minor caveat for the implementer: after the fix .inbox and .allRecordings both map to .recording with folderId nil, so a manually selected "Все" filter persists when switching between those two sections (no onChange fires) — cosmetic, both sections present the same surface. Severity P0 is arguable for a default-filter preference, but it is a real, user-requested product issue accurately evidenced.

(2) The proposed fix should NOT be applied as written: the Upload mapping is incoherent (importTypes spans audio/movie AND pdf/docx, so Upload under .item can still create recordings and vice versa); it misses other entry points that set focusedCreateMode regardless of filter — performInboxCommand (718-737, e.g. .askWai), chooseFile() (633), and the view-wide dropDestination (173, 615) — recreating the hidden-card state it tries to prevent; createPaneSubtitle (279-284) would become wrong in both EN and RU when cards are hidden; and gating the global '+' menu on a transient list filter removes functionality contrary to macOS conventions. confirmed.title: Stale-response race: rapid segment switching can show the wrong filter's rows
confirmed.evidence: load() assigns `rows = response.rows` unconditionally after the await; setSourceKind (121-125) fires a new load per segment click with no cancellation or generation guard, so a slow earlier request (e.g. Записи) can resolve last and overwrite the rows for the currently selected Все.
confirmed.fix: Add `private var loadGeneration = 0`; increment and capture `let gen = loadGeneration` at the top of load()/loadMore(), and after the await `guard gen == loadGeneration else { return }` before assigning rows/nextCursor/errorMessage.
confirmed.verifyReason: REAL, line number exact. MacInboxViewModel.swift:95 assigns `rows = response.rows` (and :96 `nextCursor`, :94/:98 `errorMessage`) unconditionally after the await; load() (85-100) has no re-entry guard (isLoading is set but never checked, unlike loadMore's `!isLoadingMore` guard at 103) and no generation/cancellation token. setSourceKind (121-125) just sets state and awaits load(). The race is reachable: MacInboxView.swift:290 spawns an independent unstructured `Task { await model.setSourceKind(next) }` per segment click (never stored/cancelled), and @MainActor isolation is released at the `await apiClient.listInbox` suspension (actor reentrancy), so rapid Записи→Все clicks run two concurrent loads and whichever resolves last overwrites rows for the currently selected segment. Worse than a flash: the stale request's nextCursor is also installed, so subsequent loadMore() paginates the wrong filter and appends mismatched rows; a stale failure can also paint errorMessage. listInbox (APIClient.swift:1313) is a plain URLSession call with no internal serialization. The proposed loadGeneration fix is the standard stale-response guard, data-race free on a @MainActor class, safe in all load/loadMore interleavings, and safe for other load() callers (configureScope:82, addDraft:160, uploadFile:248/262, newChat:285 all carry an existing `?? detail` fallback if their refresh is discarded). No copy changes, so RU/EN unaffected. Effort S accurate. Same race family also exists via configureScope (MacInboxView.swift:144-153), which the fix's load()-level guard covers too.

confirmed.title: Every reload blanks the populated list and resets scroll (table is unmounted while isLoading)
confirmed.evidence: `if model.isLoading { ProgressView() }` replaces MacInboxRowsTable for the whole fetch, so a reloadToken refresh after drag-to-folder (lines 155-159) or any detail callback tears down the NSScrollView and rebuilds it at scroll offset 0, flashing a spinner over a list the user was reading.
confirmed.fix: Change the condition to `if model.isLoading && model.rows.isEmpty` so the existing table stays mounted (and keeps scroll position) during refreshes; first load keeps the spinner.
confirmed.verifyReason: Verified at the exact cited location. MacInboxView.swift line 327 is `if model.isLoading { ProgressView() }` with MacInboxRowsTable (NSViewRepresentable wrapping NSScrollView+NSTableView, makeNSView at line 1408) only in the else branch, so any load unmounts the table and remounts it at scroll offset 0. MacInboxViewModel.load() (lines 85-100) sets isLoading=true on EVERY call and never clears rows mid-fetch, so refreshes blank a still-populated list. Triggers confirmed: reloadToken handler at lines 155-159 fires from MacContentView.moveRecordings/trashRecordings (inboxReloadToken = UUID() at lines 1306/1316 after sidebar drag-and-drop), and detail callbacks onDelete/onRestore/onMoveToFolder/onDidRename (lines 423-448) plus addDraft/uploadFile/newChat all call model.load(). MacInboxView has no .id() at its call site (MacContentView line 895), so view identity is stable and the teardown hits a live list. The Coordinator even has incremental applyRowChanges machinery commented 'keeps scroll position' that the unmount defeats — the fix aligns with existing design intent. Proposed fix `isLoading && rows.isEmpty` is correct and safe: first load keeps the spinner (rows start empty), refreshes keep the table mounted with in-place diffing, refresh-to-empty renders the empty state (rows reassigned before defer resets isLoading in the same MainActor turn), pure boolean change with no localized strings (RU/EN identical), and MacInboxRowsTable/rows are private to this file with no other consumers. Only minor trade-off: scope/source/folder switches would show stale rows during fetch instead of a spinner — standard stale-while-revalidate behavior handled by the coordinator's wholesale reloadData path, not a safety issue.

confirmed.title: 11pt orange/red status text fails WCAG AA contrast
confirmed.evidence: statusLabel.font = .systemFont(ofSize: 11, weight: .medium) with statusColor returning .systemOrange / .systemRed (1819-1828): systemOrange on a light background is ~2.2:1 and systemRed ~3.6:1, both below the 4.5:1 required for 11pt text.
confirmed.fix: Render the status as a pill: tone color at 0.15 alpha as background, text in .labelColor (or .secondaryLabelColor) at 12pt — color stays as a cue on the pill, contrast comes from the label color. Keep the tone in the accessibilityLabel via the existing text.
confirmed.verifyReason: Verified at the exact cited lines in /Users/mikwiseman/Documents/Code/wai-computer/macos/WaiComputer/WaiComputer/Features/Inbox/MacInboxView.swift. Line 1771 sets the status label to 11pt medium; line 1748 applies statusColor(for: row.statusTone) directly as textColor; lines 1819-1828 return .systemOrange (warning=processing) and .systemRed (error=failed/needsInput). Computed WCAG ratios on the light background: systemOrange #FF9500 = 2.20:1 vs white (1.86:1 vs windowBackground), systemRed #FF3B30 = 3.55:1 vs white (3.0:1 vs windowBackground) — both below the 4.5:1 required for 11pt-medium (normal-size) text; the finding's ~3.6 for red is marginally optimistic, the truth is slightly worse. Row/selection backgrounds stay light (clear over list bg; selection = accent at 0.16 alpha). The fix is sensible and contained: statusColor and StatusTone are private to this file with no other callers (grep confirmed); the status text is already in the row accessibilityLabel (line 1287, applied line 1749) so the pill+labelColor approach loses nothing for VoiceOver; pill contrast computes to ~13.4:1 (orange tint) / ~12.4:1 (red tint); RU strings ("В работе", "Нужен ввод", "Ошибка", "Архив") fit the existing <=96pt width constraint at 12pt with pill padding, with truncating-tail as guard. Switching recording A→B therefore destroys the old view and its @StateObject ViewModel and creates a fresh one; the onChangeCompat(of: recordingId) handler and the isSwitchingRecording branch are unreachable for cross-recording switches (within one identity recordingId can't change; a fresh ViewModel starts nil or with the matching prefetched detail). On teardown nothing retains the old ViewModel (playback completion Task captures [weak self] at ViewModel line 363; loadTask is cancelled in onDisappear, view lines 107-108; .task(id:) auto-cancels), so it deallocates, releasing the only strong reference to the AVAudioPlayer (line 64) — a deallocated AVAudioPlayer stops playing. So recording A's summary audio stops when switching to B, and B's "Play Audio" button is correct, not a missing stop affordance. The proposed fix would be harmless defensive code (private single-caller helper, no RU/EN copy involved) but patches a dead path; the P1 severity is unwarranted.

p2.title: Restore and Delete never get text labels, even in the wide layout where every other action is labeled

p2.title: Header metadata mixes 11pt and 13pt type, and durations over an hour render as "90:23"
p2.evidence: On one row the date uses Typography.caption (11pt, line 331) while duration uses Typography.mono (13pt) — and `String(format: "%d:%02d", mins, secs)` shows a 90-minute meeting as "90:23", which sits ambiguously next to the time-of-day from the date string.

p2.title: Section rhythm built from `Spacing.xl / 2` arithmetic instead of tokens
p2.evidence: `.padding(.bottom, Spacing.xl + Spacing.xl / 2)` plus three `Spacing.xl / 2` paddings (576, 580, 591) compute 36 and 12 inline — 12 already exists as Spacing.md, and 36 is off the 8pt grid the Spacing enum defines.

p2.title: Export menu items use inconsistent grammar in both languages
p2.evidence: Three items start with "Export …" but the fourth is `t("Plain Text + timestamps (.txt)", "Текст с тайм-кодами (.txt)")`; RU also mixes transliteration styles — "Экспорт TXT (.txt)" (redundant TXT+.txt) next to "Текст с тайм-кодами".

confirmed.title: Disk-full kills the audio loop but UI keeps claiming 'Recording'
confirmed.evidence: On write failure the task logs "Disk write failed — stopping recording to preserve data", sets `self.error = diskFullMessage` and `return`s — but phase stays `.recording`, the timer keeps ticking, and the red dot keeps pulsing while zero audio is captured; the alert copy ('Recording stopped to preserve what was captured') is false.
confirmed.fix: After setting the error inside `MainActor.run`, spawn `Task { await self.stopRecording() }` and then return from the audio loop (fire-and-forget, NOT awaited inline — stopRecording awaits this very task's result and would deadlock). That actually finalizes the WAV and flips phase to .finalizing → .idle, matching the alert copy.
confirmed.verifyReason: VERIFIED REAL at the exact cited location. In MacRecordingViewModel.swift the detached audio loop (lines 433-469) handles a failed `fileWriter.writeEncodedPCM` at lines 446-451: it logs "Disk write failed — stopping recording to preserve data" (line 448, exactly as cited), sets `self.error = diskFullMessage` via MainActor.run, and `return`s from the task — never calling `stopRecording()` or `setPhase`. Every downstream claim checks out: (1) phase stays `.recording` (set at line 488; only `setPhase` at line 1347 mutates it, and no error-path caller exists); (2) the timer keeps ticking — `startTimer()` (line 1243) increments `duration` every second gated only on `!isPaused`, not on phase or loop health; (3) the red dot keeps pulsing — LiveRecordingView.swift lines 207-211 render `Circle().fill(Palette.recording)` (= `Color.red`, DesignSystem.swift:282) with `PulseModifier` whenever `phase == .recording`, and `statusText` shows "Recording"/"Идет запись"; (4) the alert copy is false in BOTH languages ("Recording stopped to preserve what was captured." / "Запись остановлена для сохранения того, что успели записать.") — the alert in MacContentView.swift lines 387-416 offers only OK → `clearError()`, so nothing actually stops; the mic/capture stays hot while zero audio is written to disk or sent to the realtime WS. The unfinalized WAV also risks a corrupt header if the app quits. The proposed fix is sensible and its deadlock rationale is code-verified: `stopRecording()`'s cleanup task does `_ = await sendingTask?.result` (line 577) on this very audio task and `stopRecording` awaits that cleanup (`await task.value`, line 648), so an inline `await self.stopRecording()` from inside the loop would form a circular await (audioTask → stopRecording → cleanupTask → audioTask.result) — fire-and-forget `Task { await self.stopRecording() }` then `return` is the correct shape. After it, `stopRecording` passes its `guard phase == .recording`, flips to `.finalizing`, stops capture, finalizes the WAV (line 580), persists via the background-sync path, and lands at `.idle` — making the existing EN and RU copy true without touching strings. Safe for other callers: `stopRecording` is reentrancy-guarded on MainActor (`.finalizing` → await cleanupTask; `guard .recording`), so a concurrent user stop serializes cleanly, and grep confirms this disk-full path/message exists nowhere else. One minor edge the fix should note: if the write fails in the milliseconds before `setPhase(.recording)` (line 488), the spawned stop no-ops on its phase guard and start still flips to `.recording` with a dead loop — already broken today, not a regression, but ideally the start path should also check whether audioTask already finished. P0 severity is justified: silent, open-ended data loss while the UI affirmatively claims recording.

confirmed.title: Paused recording still shows the pulsing red 'recording' dot
confirmed.evidence: `} else if recordingVM.phase == .recording { Circle().fill(Palette.recording) … .modifier(PulseModifier())` — `isPaused` keeps phase == .recording, so while the header text says 'Пауза' the dot keeps pulsing red, the universal signal for active capture.
confirmed.fix: Add a paused branch before the recording one: `if recordingVM.phase == .recording && recordingVM.isPaused` show a static `Image(systemName: "pause.fill")` (or a non-pulsing `Palette.textTertiary` circle) at 12×12; keep the pulsing red dot only for active, unpaused capture.
confirmed.verifyReason: VERIFIED REAL at the exact cited line. LiveRecordingView.swift:207-211: `} else if recordingVM.phase == .recording { Circle().fill(Palette.recording) ... .modifier(PulseModifier()) }` — Palette.recording is Color.red (DesignSystem.swift:282) and PulseModifier (same file, line 353) is a repeating opacity pulse. Pause genuinely does not change phase: MacRecordingPhase (MacRecordingViewModel.swift:61-66) has no .paused case, and pauseRecording() (768-782) only sets isPaused=true while phase stays .recording; connectionState is untouched, so the line-207 branch keeps rendering the pulsing red dot while paused. Meanwhile statusText (MacRecordingViewModel.swift:165-169) returns "Paused"/"Пауза" for .recording+isPaused and is rendered right next to the dot (LiveRecordingView.swift:218) — the contradictory signal is real in both EN and RU. The fix is sensible and safe: isPaused is @Published private(set) and readable from the view (the view already uses the equivalent canResumeRecording == phase==.recording && isPaused at line 138); the change is icon-only with no new strings, so RU/EN-safe; PulseModifier is used nowhere outside this file, and Palette.recording is not modified, so no other callers are affected. Minor nits only: (a) placing the paused branch after the reconnecting branch (as worded) leaves a pulsing orange dot if a pause coincides with reconnection — acceptable edge; (b) a 12x12 .frame won't scale an SF Symbol glyph (use .font/.imageScale), though the alternative static textTertiary 12x12 circle is exactly right. Note MenuBarView.swift:58 has a milder static-red-dot-while-paused cousin, outside this finding's scope.

confirmed.title: White text on orange banners fails WCAG AA (~2.2:1)
confirmed.evidence: Both banners use `.foregroundStyle(.white)` / `.white.opacity(0.7…0.85)` on `.background(Color.orange)` — white on systemOrange is ≈2.2:1 for 11–12pt Typography.label/caption text; DesignSystem.swift's own `onAccentColor` comment documents that white fails on amber (≈2.1–3.3:1).
confirmed.fix: Switch banner foregrounds to black: `.foregroundStyle(.black)` for primary text/icon and `.black.opacity(0.7)` for the secondary line (lines 22, 26, 38, 42, 48) — black on systemOrange is ≈9.5:1. Apply to both the reconnection and offline banners.
confirmed.verifyReason: Verified at the cited location. LiveRecordingView.swift lines 22/26 (reconnection banner) and 38/42/48 (offline banner) use .white / .white.opacity(0.7–0.85) foregrounds over .background(Color.orange) at lines 30/54, with Typography.label (12pt) and caption (11pt) — normal text needing 4.5:1. Computed contrast: white on systemOrange = 2.20:1 light / 2.06:1 dark (claim's ~2.2:1 accurate; the 0.7-opacity secondary blend is 1.72:1, worse). The evidence's DesignSystem citation is verbatim real: Core/DesignSystem.swift:113-115 onAccentColor comment says hardcoded white fails WCAG AA on amber (~2.1–3.3:1). Fix verified sensible and safe: black on systemOrange = 9.55:1 light / 10.22:1 dark (matches ≈9.5:1 claim), .black.opacity(0.7) blend = 5.68:1 still passes AA; the banners are view-local (accessibility IDs referenced nowhere else, view only used by MacContentView), the change is language-independent (RU/EN both plain Text), and Color.orange is near-identical in light/dark so fixed .black has no dark-mode regression. Hardcoded .black is correct here (background is hardcoded orange, not the user accent, so Palette.onAccent would be wrong). Only omission: the default-styled ProgressView spinner at line 18 isn't covered by the fix, but it isn't part of the claim.

confirmed.title: Stop button morphs into a status label, duplicating the header status
confirmed.evidence: `Text(recordingVM.canStopRecording ? t("Stop", "Остановить") : recordingVM.statusText)` — during preparing/finalizing the disabled gray button reads 'Готовим запись'/'Сохраняем расшифровку...' with a stop.fill icon, while the header (line 218) already shows the identical `recordingVM.statusText`; the same string renders twice and the button width jumps from 150pt to fit the long label.
confirmed.fix: Keep the button label fixed: `Text(t("Stop", "Остановить"))`. Disabled state + the header status already communicate phase; drop the `.tint(… : .gray)` ternary too since `.disabled` dims borderedProminent on its own.
confirmed.verifyReason: Verified exactly at the cited locations. LiveRecordingView.swift:153-156 renders Text(recordingVM.canStopRecording ? t("Stop", "Остановить") : recordingVM.statusText) inside the Stop button with a stop.fill icon (line 159), .frame(minWidth: 150) (line 162), .tint(canStop ? .red : .gray) (line 166), .disabled (line 167). MacRecordingViewModel.swift:143 defines canStopRecording = (phase == .recording), and statusText (lines 163-171) returns "Готовим запись"/"Preparing recording" for .preparing and "Сохраняем расшифровку..."/"Saving transcript..." for .finalizing — the exact strings claimed. The header at line 218 shows the identical recordingVM.statusText (with a spinner, lines 212-216), so the string genuinely renders twice during preparing/finalizing, and minWidth-only + lineLimit(1) makes the button widen to fit the long label (the "from 150pt" figure is approximate but the jump is real). The fix is sensible and safe: it changes only this view's button label; statusText stays intact for the header, MenuBarView (lines 23, 113), and unit tests (statusText(language:) assertions); UI tests find the button via the accessibility identifier "stop-recording-button", not label text; both RU and EN strings already exist via t(); and .disabled dims borderedProminent on macOS so the gray tint ternary is redundant. Caveat: MenuBarView.swift:113 duplicates the same morphing-label pattern, so the full fix should also cover it — this confirms rather than contradicts the finding.

confirmed.title: Russian discard flow uses two different verbs for one action
confirmed.evidence: Trigger button: `t("Discard", "Не сохранять")` (line 125), but the dialog it opens is titled `t("Discard this recording?", "Удалить эту запись?")` (line 178) with confirm `t("Discard", "Удалить запись")` (line 185) — EN is consistently 'Discard', RU jumps from 'don't save' to 'delete' mid-flow.
confirmed.fix: Unify RU on one verb family: title `"Не сохранять запись?"`, confirm `"Не сохранять"`, trigger stays `"Не сохранять"` (the deletion consequence is already spelled out in the dialog message line 194).
confirmed.verifyReason: REAL — verified at the exact cited lines in /Users/mikwiseman/Documents/Code/wai-computer/macos/WaiComputer/WaiComputer/Features/Recording/LiveRecordingView.swift. Line 125: trigger button label t("Discard", "Не сохранять"); line 178: confirmationDialog title t("Discard this recording?", "Удалить эту запись?"); line 185: destructive confirm Text(t("Discard", "Удалить запись")); lines 192–195: dialog message "Аудио и расшифровка будут удалены. Восстановить их нельзя." (RU on line 194, as cited). EN is consistently "Discard" across all three; RU switches verb family mid-flow from "не сохранять" (don't save) to "удалить" (delete) — the inconsistency is exactly as described. The proposed fix is sensible and safe: (1) EN untouched; (2) "Не сохранять" as a red destructive confirm paired with "Отмена" mirrors the native macOS Russian "Don't Save" dialog pattern, and the deletion consequence stays spelled out in the line-194 message, so severity isn't lost; (3) the strings are inline in this view's private t() helper — no shared constants, no other callers; (4) no tests assert on these label texts (UI hooks use accessibilityIdentifiers "discard-recording-button"/"discard-recording-confirm"); (5) the trash/permanent-delete flows (MacRecordingDetailView.swift:535) correctly keep "Удалить" since those delete saved recordings — unifying live-discard on "Не сохранять" creates a clean semantic split (abandon in-progress vs delete saved). Bonus precedent: iOS's identical flow already uses "Не сохранять" for the confirm (ios/WaiComputer/WaiComputer/Features/Recording/RecordingView.swift:274) while keeping the mixed title "Удалить эту запись?" (line 267) — the macOS fix converges with iOS on the confirm verb; applying the same title change on iOS would be a sensible follow-up but doesn't affect this fix's safety. Minor calibration note: as a copy-consistency issue in a destructive flow, P1 is defensible but P2 would also fit; the finding itself is accurate.

confirmed.title: Recording error alert matches English substrings — RU users never get the settings deep-link buttons
confirmed.evidence: `if message.contains("Microphone permission")` / `message.contains("Audio Capture") || message.contains("System audio")` — but `recordingViewModel.error` is localized; the RU strings ('Доступ к микрофону запрещен…', 'Не удалось запустить захват звука Mac…') contain none of those substrings, so Russian users get a bare OK instead of 'Открыть настройки микрофона' on the first-run permission path.
confirmed.fix: Publish a typed error kind from MacRecordingViewModel (e.g. `enum RecordingErrorKind { case micPermission, systemAudio, general }` set alongside the message at lines 322 and 1568) and switch the alert buttons on that enum instead of substring-matching localized copy.
confirmed.verifyReason: REAL, verified end-to-end at the exact cited line. MacContentView.swift:395 gates the recording-error alert buttons on `message.contains("Microphone permission")` and :403 on `contains("Audio Capture") || contains("System audio")`, but the message comes from MacRecordingViewModel where it is localized via LanguageManager (OnboardingL10n.text): the mic error (MacRecordingViewModel.swift:322-325) is RU "Доступ к микрофону запрещен…" and the system-audio error (set at :499 via recordingErrorMessage → RecordingCopy.systemAudioCaptureUnavailableMessage, SystemAudioWarningPolicy.swift:17-23, returned at MacRecordingViewModel.swift:1568/1573) is RU "Не удалось запустить захват звука Mac…" — neither RU string contains any matched English substring, so RU (and followSystem-with-ru-locale) users fall to the bare OK branch and never see "Открыть настройки микрофона"/"Открыть системные настройки". EN strings do contain the substrings, so EN is unaffected. SystemAudioWarningPolicyTests.swift:79-88 even asserts the RU string does NOT contain "System audio capture", so the divergence is by-design and permanent. The proposed fix (publish a typed RecordingErrorKind set alongside the message at :322 and the system-audio path :499/:1568, switch the alert on the enum) is the correct decoupling and is safe: grep shows the alert is the only consumer of recordingViewModel.error, UserFacingErrorFormatter produces none of the matched substrings (so all other error paths map cleanly to .general with unchanged behavior), and the change is additive for both languages. One scope gap worth carrying into the fix: the identical bug exists at MacContentView.swift:42 for the Dictation Error alert (dictationManager.error matched against "Microphone permission" while DictationCopy.microphonePermissionDenied RU is "Нет доступа к микрофону…", DictationCopy.swift:51-58) — the typed-kind approach should be applied to DictationManager as well.

confirmed.title: NewRecordingView advertises ⌘N and ⌘I — ⌘N does something else, ⌘I does nothing
confirmed.evidence: `shortcut: "⌘N"` on the Record row and `shortcut: "⌘I"` on Import (line 53) — but ⌘N is bound to 'New Inbox Item' / showCreatePane (WaiComputerMacApp.swift:189), not this row's immediate `onStartRecording`, and no ⌘I binding exists anywhere in the app (Record Now is ⇧⌘R, Upload File is ⌥⌘U).
confirmed.fix: Show the real bindings — change the hints to "⇧⌘R" and "⌥⌘U" — or, better, attach `.keyboardShortcut("r", modifiers: [.command, .shift])` / a new `.keyboardShortcut("i", modifiers: .command)` to these Buttons so the printed hints are literally true on this screen.
confirmed.verifyReason: Verified at the exact cited lines. NewRecordingView.swift:38 prints "⌘N" on the Record row and :53 prints "⌘I" on Import, but RecordingOptionRow renders the shortcut as decorative Text only (line 106) — no .keyboardShortcut is attached to either Button. ⌘N is in fact bound at WaiComputerMacApp.swift:189 to "New Inbox Item" → postInboxCommand(.showCreatePane), which MacInboxView.swift:720-722 handles by opening the inbox create pane — not this row's onStartRecording. An exhaustive grep of all .keyboardShortcut uses plus non-SwiftUI key handling (keyEquivalent/addLocalMonitor/onKeyPress) confirms no ⌘I binding exists anywhere (only an Escape handler and dictation PTT monitors). Record Now is ⇧⌘R (:200-203) and Upload File is ⌥⌘U (:206-209), exactly as the finding states. So both printed hints are false: ⌘N does something else (and is even disabled while unauthenticated/recording), ⌘I does nothing. The fix is sensible and safe: ⇧⌘R's .recordNow path calls MacInboxView's onStartRecording which MacContentView.swift:904-905 wires to the identical startRecording(type:.meeting,inputSource:.dual,folderId:) closure as this view's row (:976), and ⌘I is free app-wide. The shortcut strings are language-neutral glyphs outside the t() RU/EN helper, RecordingOptionRow is private to the file, and NewRecordingView has a single call site (MacContentView.swift:975), so no other callers or locales are affected. Two minor implementation caveats (don't invalidate the finding): the hint-only variant's ⌥⌘U is approximate for Import since the menu's Upload File runs MacInboxView.chooseFile() rather than this view's importAudioFile() — the finding's preferred variant (attach a real new ⌘I binding) is cleaner; and attaching ⇧⌘R to the button duplicates the menu's shortcut while the view is visible (same action, low risk).

confirmed.title: Live transcript force-scrolls to bottom on every event — can't read back or select text mid-meeting
confirmed.evidence: `.onChangeCompat(of: recordingVM.currentTranscript) { … proxy.scrollTo("transcript-bottom", anchor: .bottom) }` fires unconditionally with animation on every interim update (several per second during speech), yanking the user back to the bottom the moment they scroll up — even though `.textSelection(.enabled)` explicitly invites selecting/copying earlier text.
confirmed.fix: Track whether the user is pinned to the bottom (a `@State isPinnedToBottom` updated from scroll geometry, or detecting upward scroll via NSScrollView's willStartLiveScroll notification); only auto-scroll when pinned, and show a small 'Jump to latest' / «К последним словам» pill when suspended.
confirmed.verifyReason: VERIFIED REAL at the exact cited line. LiveRecordingView.swift:99-103 contains `.onChangeCompat(of: recordingVM.currentTranscript) { _, _ in withAnimation { proxy.scrollTo("transcript-bottom", anchor: .bottom) } }` — unconditional, animated, with no pinned-to-bottom state or user-scroll detection anywhere in the macOS target. The frequency claim is corroborated by the codebase itself: MacRecordingViewModel.handleWebSocketEvent (lines 1369-1385) reassigns currentTranscript on EVERY interim and final transcript event, and the VM's own comment says these run "several times a second during speech"; onChangeCompat is a thin native-onChange wrapper firing per change. `.textSelection(.enabled)` is indeed on both committed (line 79) and interim (line 86) text, so the UI invites selection that the forced scroll then breaks. The proposed fix (pin-to-bottom tracking + 'Jump to latest' pill, auto-scroll only when pinned) is the standard live-feed pattern, is local to this view (the shared onChangeCompat helper and the "transcript-bottom" anchor's only other user — iOS RecordingView.swift:141/151, which has its own copy of the bug — are untouched), and is RU/EN-safe via the view's existing t(en, ru) helper (line 337). One implementation caveat: the scroll-geometry variant (onScrollGeometryChange) is macOS 15+ while the app floor is macOS 13, so use the NSScrollView willStartLiveScroll/notification or preference-key approach the finding already offers. Effort M is fair.

p2.title: Completion transition resurrects the 'Встреча' type badge that was removed from the live header
p2.evidence: `Text(recordingTypeLabel(transition.recordingType))` in CompletedRecordingTransitionView — every start hardcodes `type: .meeting` (MacContentView:905, 976), so after every stop an accent-colored 'Встреча/Meeting' badge appears; LiveRecordingView:238's comment documents this exact badge was removed because 'every recording was typed meeting, so the badge was always-on and confusing'.

p2.title: 'Records your mic and computer audio' is untrue below macOS 14.2
p2.evidence: Subtitle `t("Records your mic and computer audio", "Записывает микрофон и звук компьютера")` is unconditional, but `startRecording` coerces `inputSource` to `.microphone` when `SystemAudioGate.isSupported` is false (MacRecordingViewModel.swift:231) — the app's floor is macOS 13.0 while system audio is gated 14.2+, so 13.x–14.1 users are promised system audio they won't get.

p2.title: Interim transcript's accessibilityLabel hides the actual words from VoiceOver
p2.evidence: `.accessibilityLabel(t("Interim transcript — may change as you speak", …))` on the interim `Text` replaces its content for assistive tech — VoiceOver users hear the same static explanation over and over and never the interim words themselves.
p2.fix: Change `.accessibilityLabel(...)` to `.accessibilityHint(...)` so VoiceOver reads the interim text first and the 'may change' caveat after it.

p2.title: Raw Color.orange used in 3 places instead of a warning token
p2.evidence: `.background(Color.orange)` (lines 30, 54) and `Circle().fill(Color.orange)` (line 204) hardcode the warning color while every other recording color routes through Palette (`Palette.recording`, `Palette.accent`).

p2.title: Mixed '...' (ASCII) and '…' (ellipsis) across recording status strings
p2.evidence: VM strings use three periods — `t("Saving transcript...", "Сохраняем расшифровку...")` (171), 'Listening...' (212), 'Preparing audio capture...' (48) — while LiveRecordingView's banner uses the proper ellipsis: `t("Reconnecting…", "Переподключение…")` (line 20); both render in the same header area.

confirmed.title: Permanent deletion in Trash has no confirmation on any list path — one Delete keypress destroys recordings
confirmed.evidence: .onDeleteCommand { ... if isTrash { onPermanentDelete(ids) } } fires straight into deletion; same for the toolbar button (MacContentView.swift:305-312), context menu (RecordingListView.swift:39), and BulkSelectionDetailView (MacContentView.swift:1571) — yet the detail view DOES confirm (MacRecordingDetailView.swift:534), so the same action is guarded in one place and instant in four others. Delete key means safe 'move to trash' everywhere else in the app, but in Trash it irreversibly destroys data with no dialog and no undo.
confirmed.fix: Add an idsPendingPermanentDelete: [String]? state in MacMainView with a confirmationDialog reusing the existing copy t("Delete Permanently", "Удалить навсегда") from MacRecordingDetailView:534-538, and route all four list entry points (onDeleteCommand, toolbar, context menu, bulk view) through it before calling permanentlyDeleteRecordings.
confirmed.verifyReason: Verified at every cited location. RecordingListView.swift:77-85: .onDeleteCommand calls onPermanentDelete(ids) directly when isTrash (line 77 is the modifier, call at 81); non-trash branch calls onTrash — so Delete key is recoverable everywhere except Trash, where it is irreversible. RecordingListView.swift:39-41: context-menu "Delete Permanently" fires onPermanentDelete(contextSelection) directly. MacContentView.swift:305-312: Trash toolbar button calls permanentlyDeleteSelectedRecordings() directly. MacContentView.swift:1571 (+ wiring at 926): BulkSelectionDetailView button calls permanentlyDeleteSelectedRecordings directly. All four funnel into permanentlyDeleteRecordings(_:) (MacContentView.swift:1337-1344) which immediately calls MacLibraryViewModel.permanentlyDeleteRecordings → apiClient.deleteRecording(permanent: true) per id — no confirmationDialog/alert guards this path (the only dialogs in MacContentView are dictation/import/recording errors and delete-folder), and no undoManager/registerUndo exists in the macOS target. Meanwhile MacRecordingDetailView.swift:525-557 guards the same action with a confirmationDialog (line 534) titled "Delete this recording permanently?"/"Удалить запись навсегда?" with "This action cannot be undone."/"Это действие нельзя отменить." — exactly the inconsistency claimed. The fix is sensible: MacMainView (MacContentView.swift:91) is the correct owner — it contains all four entry points and both funnel functions, so a single idsPendingPermanentDelete state + confirmationDialog gating permanentlyDeleteRecordings(_:) covers all four paths with one dialog; RU/EN safe via the existing t() helper and already-translated copy; the detail view uses a separate already-guarded method (MacRecordingDetailViewModel.deleteRecording) and no other caller of the view model method exists, so no double-confirmation or breakage. Minor note only: the reused single-recording dialog title needs a pluralized variant for multi-selection.

confirmed.title: Dragging a recording out of Trash onto Inbox or a folder silently does nothing useful (doesn't restore)
confirmed.evidence: .draggable(RecordingDragItem(recordingId: recording.id)) is unconditional (trash rows included), but the sidebar Inbox drop runs handleRecordingDrop(items) { moveRecordings([$0], to: nil) } (MacContentView.swift:649) and MacLibraryViewModel.moveRecordings (line 160) only issues a .move action — the item stays trashed, never appears in the destination (folder views filter trashed=false), and may acquire an invisible folder assignment. The obvious user intent 'drag out of Trash = restore' fails with zero feedback.
confirmed.fix: In the sidebar drop handlers, check whether the dragged id is in libraryViewModel.trashedRecordings; if so call restoreRecordings first, then moveRecordings to the target folder. Alternatively gate .draggable on !isTrash so the dead gesture can't start.
confirmed.verifyReason: Verified at every layer. (1) RecordingListView.swift:27 — .draggable(RecordingDragItem(recordingId:)) is unconditional; isTrash (line 9) never gates it, and the Trash section renders this exact view (MacContentView.swift:823, isTrash: isTrashSection, rows from filteredRecordings(trashed: true)), so trash rows can start a drag. (2) MacContentView.swift:648-649 Inbox drop runs handleRecordingDrop { moveRecordings([$0], to: nil) } exactly as cited; folder drop at 756-757 does moveRecordings to folder.id. (3) MacLibraryViewModel.swift:160 moveRecordings issues only action .move via runBulkOperation. (4) Backend recordings.py:1447-1448 — "move" sets only folder_id, never clears deleted_at, and the bulk fetch (1429-1435) does not exclude trashed rows, so the call counts as processed with no error: zero feedback. (5) recordings.py:1196-1199 — active listing requires deleted_at IS NULL, so the still-trashed item never appears in Inbox/folder views (which draw from the active recordings array); it stays visible in Trash (trash filter passes folderId nil), and a folder drop leaves an invisible folder_id that surfaces only on a later explicit Restore. All cited line numbers are accurate. The fix is sensible and safe: gating .draggable on !isTrash affects only trash rows (the only other RecordingDragItem source, MacInboxView.swift:1599-1610, serves the non-trash inbox table; the trash context menu already hides Move and offers Restore), and the restore-then-move variant is viable since trashedRecordings is accessible — with one implementation caveat: MacContentView's restoreRecordings/moveRecordings each spawn independent Tasks, so the restore must be awaited before the move (single Task or a combined view-model method) to avoid racing two bulk operations. No copy changes involved, so RU/EN behave identically.

confirmed.title: Auth screen: pressing Return never submits — email/password flow requires a mouse click
confirmed.evidence: The submit Button(action: submit) has no .keyboardShortcut(.defaultAction) and none of the TextField/SecureField inputs (lines 1815-1836) have .onSubmit, so on the app's very first screen typing email → tab → password → Return does nothing.

MacContentView.swift:1785 (inside MacAuthView, struct at 1695) is the submit Button(action: submit) with buttonStyle/disabled/accessibilityIdentifier but no .keyboardShortcut(.defaultAction); repo grep confirms defaultAction exists only in Onboarding files. The email TextField (1815-1820) and both SecureFields (1823-1836) have no .onSubmit — the file's only onSubmit usages (435, 458, 1483, 1508) belong to the unrelated FolderNameSheet. No Form, no Return onKeyPress (DesignSystem helper handles .escape only), and no NSWindow keyEquivalent, so with a custom ButtonStyle Return triggers nothing: the email→tab→password→Return dead-end is real. Minor correction to the framing: the strictly first screen on a fresh install is OnboardingView(phase: .preAuth) (MacContentView.swift:13-14); MacAuthView is the first screen on every subsequent logged-out launch — substance unaffected (onboarding buttons DO have .defaultAction, underscoring the inconsistency). The proposed fix is sensible and safe: the button stays gated by .disabled(!isFormValid || isLoading) so the shortcut cannot fire invalid submits (including the magicLinkSent state where isFormValid is false), there is no competing .defaultAction on the screen, the suggested onSubmit is isFormValid-guarded to match, the change adds no user-facing strings so RU/EN localization via t() is untouched, and MacAuthView has a single call site (MacContentView.swift:19) with no shared components modified.

confirmed.title: Folder name sheet ignores Return and Esc — keyboard folder creation (⇧⌘N) dead-ends

confirmed.title: Library error banner is white text on Color.orange — ~2.2:1 contrast, fails WCAG AA

InlineLibraryErrorBanner (MacContentView.swift:1439-1470) uses .foregroundStyle(Color.white) on the icon (1446) and 13pt message text (1450, Typography.bodySmall = .system(size: 13) per DesignSystem.swift:40), plus white.opacity(0.9) on the dismiss icon (1457), all over .background(Color.orange) at line 1464 (cited line is exact). No custom Color.orange override exists in the app or WaiComputerKit, so it resolves to systemOrange (#FF9500 light): white contrast = ~2.20:1, failing WCAG AA for normal text (4.5:1) and even the 3:1 large-text bar. One implementation nuance: reuse the luminance-pick LOGIC of onAccentColor against the banner's own orange background (or hardcode .black) — do NOT substitute Palette.onAccent itself, since it computes against the user's accent color, not orange; the finding's wording already gets this right.

confirmed.title: Menu bar 'Recent' list loads once and goes stale — never loads at all if the user signs in after first open
confirmed.evidence: .task { if appState.isAuthenticated { await loadRecentRecordings() } } runs once per view lifetime; with .menuBarExtraStyle(.window) the content persists, and the only other refresh is inside the menu's own Stop button (line 107). Recordings made from the main window never appear, and a user signed out at first open gets a permanently empty 'RECENT'/'НЕДАВНИЕ' section.

MenuBarView.swift:46-50 has a plain `.task` (no id:) guarded by `appState.isAuthenticated`, and WaiComputerMacApp.swift:282-298 hosts MenuBarView in `MenuBarExtra{...}.menuBarExtraStyle(.window)`, whose content persists across menu opens — so the task runs once per app lifetime. The only two call sites of loadRecentRecordings() are line 48 (.task) and line 107 (menu's own Stop button); no onReceive/onChange observers exist in the view. Consequences as described: (a) authenticated-at-launch users get a list that goes stale — recordings stopped from the main window or background-synced never appear; (b) a user unauthenticated at first task run sees the body flip to authenticatedMenu reactively (lines 39-43) but recentRecordings (@State, line 11) stays [], showing 'No recordings yet'/'Записей пока нет' (line 208) until they stop a live recording from the menu itself — the one escape hatch the finding already names. The fix is sensible and safe: .task(id: appState.isAuthenticated) is the standard pattern (sign-out re-run is harmless — guard skips, error path already resets to []); onChangeCompat exists in WaiComputerKit (Compat/OnChangeCompat.swift) and is used app-wide; .pendingRecordingSyncDidFinish exists (PendingRecordingSyncCoordinator.swift:6) and MacMainView (MacContentView.swift:91, observer at :565 → reloadLibrary()) observes it exactly as claimed. confirmed.title: Menu bar dates use system locale, not the in-app language — mixed-language UI
confirmed.evidence: recording.createdAt.formatted(date: .abbreviated, time: .omitted) (and last.timestamp.formatted(...) at line 261) bypass LanguageManager, so with app language = Russian on an English system the menu shows 'Jun 10' next to 'НЕДАВНИЕ' — the exact mixed-language bug MacDateFormatting.string(language:) was built to prevent (RecordingListView.swift:138 uses it correctly).
confirmed.fix: Replace both .formatted calls with MacDateFormatting.string(from:dateStyle:.medium/.short, timeStyle:, language: languageManager.current), matching RecordingRowView.
confirmed.verifyReason: VERIFIED REAL at the exact cited lines. MenuBarView.swift:224 contains `Text(recording.createdAt.formatted(date: .abbreviated, time: .omitted))` and :261 contains `Text(last.timestamp.formatted(date: .abbreviated, time: .shortened))` — both are Foundation Date.formatted() calls that format with the system locale and return a String, ignoring LanguageManager and the SwiftUI environment locale. The adjacent header (line 203) is `t("RECENT", "НЕДАВНИЕ")` via LanguageManager, so app-language=RU on an EN system genuinely shows 'НЕДАВНИЕ' next to 'Jun 10' (and the dictation section header 'ДИКТОВКА' next to an EN date+time). Adversarial checks: (a) the MenuBarExtra scene (WaiComputerMacApp.swift:282-290) injects languageManager but applies NO `.environment(\.locale, ...)` override — unlike MacContentView.swift:917 / MacInboxView.swift:482 — and such an override wouldn't affect Foundation .formatted() anyway; (b) the cited correct pattern is confirmed: RecordingListView.swift:138 uses MacDateFormatting.string(from:dateStyle:.medium,timeStyle:.short,language:languageManager.current). The fix is sensible and safe: MacDateFormatting.string(from:dateStyle:timeStyle:language:) exists at DesignSystem.swift:216+ with exactly that signature; .followSystem maps to Locale.current so follow-system users see identical output; languageManager is already an @EnvironmentObject in MenuBarView with @Published current, so dates re-render live on language switch; the helper is the established pattern (7+ existing callers) with thread-safe formatter caching, and only these two call sites change — no other callers of shared code are affected. Minor precision note on the fix: line 224 should use timeStyle:.none (date-only today) and line 261 dateStyle:.medium,timeStyle:.short; the finding's 'dateStyle:.medium/.short' phrasing is loose but directionally correct. P1/l10n severity is consistent with this codebase's documented 'mixed-language UI' gotcha that MacDateFormatting was built to prevent.

confirmed.title: Sidebar selection is invisible to VoiceOver — custom buttons never announce the selected section

Palette.accent.opacity(0.15) : Color.clear) — a purely visual cue with no .isSelected trait, so VoiceOver reads every section identically and a blind user cannot tell where they are; arrow-key traversal of the sidebar is also lost versus a native List selection.

No .accessibilityAddTraits/.isSelected/.accessibilityValue exists anywhere in the file, and SidebarRowLabel (line 1351) never receives selection, so VoiceOver reads selected and unselected rows identically. The sidebar is a plain `List {` (line 639) with no selection: binding, so native arrow-key selection traversal is indeed absent. The fix is sound and safe for all callers (inbox/trash/folders/history/dictionary/search/settings all route through sidebarRow; the New Folder action button is separate and untouched) and RU/EN-safe since the .isSelected trait announcement is system-localized; SidebarSection: Hashable (line 131) plus optional selectedSection (line 104) make the longer-term List(selection:) migration feasible. Only caveats: an added accessibilityLabel with drop-target wording must use t() for RU/EN, and a List(selection:) migration must preserve .dropDestination/drop-tint behavior — consistent with the finding's own "longer term" framing.

p2.title: The '+' toolbar button instantly starts a live recording — and is shown even in Trash

OnboardingView.swift:202 is exactly `if !isSandboxPage {` around the footer primary button (`.keyboardShortcut(.defaultAction)`, line 209), so on voiceSetup the footer renders Back + "Skip" + default-action "Continue". voiceSetup is always the last page of the postAuth phase ([.sandbox, .voiceSetup], OnboardingPage.swift:43-44; presented from MacContentView.swift:21), so Continue → handlePrimaryTap → advanceOrComplete → completeOnboarding() — the only enrollVoice upload path is the slide's own submit() (OnboardingVoiceSetupSlide.swift:144-170), and the temp WAV is already deleted after loadRecordedData (313-318), so a recorded take (max 20s, read-aloud prompt) is silently dropped from memory with no warning. The competing-CTA claim is also accurate: with a recorded take the screen shows slide "Skip for now"/"Re-record"/"Use this take" (OnboardingVoiceSetupSlide.swift:112-125, cited 113-125 correct) plus footer Back/"Skip"/"Continue", and the two skips are differently worded in both EN ("Skip" vs "Skip for now") and RU ("Пропустить" vs "Пропустить пока") because line 193's ternary (isPermissionPage || isSandboxPage) is false on voiceSetup. The fix is sensible and safe: an additive ownsFooterCTA property breaks no other callers (OnboardingPage used only in onboarding code, no tests reference voiceSetup), the slide always offers a forward path ("Skip for now" → onAdvance in all recorder states), and all copy goes through bilingual OnboardingL10n so RU/EN are covered. One nuance: the footer "Skip" button at line 193 is OUTSIDE the `if !isSandboxPage` block, so the literal condition swap suppresses only Continue (fixing the P0 silent-discard); fully removing the dual-skip wording also requires gating that skip row (the same skip duplication already exists on the sandbox page). The alternative fix (footer Continue calls submit() when recorded && hasMinimumDuration) needs the recorder state lifted out of the slide's @StateObject — feasible, effort S. Severity P0 is arguable (optional enrollment step; P1 defensible), but the described behavior is real exactly as cited.

confirmed.title: Russian copy mixes formal «вы» and informal «ты» registers within the same flow — even on one slide

(1) OnboardingWelcomeSlide.swift:29 has the вы-form subtitle «Записывайте, загружайте файлы, сохраняйте ссылки и общайтесь с Wai из одного Инбокса…», and line 54 — exactly 25 lines below on the SAME slide — has ты-form «Выбери язык приложения». rejected.title: Sandbox is a dead end when Microphone/Accessibility were skipped — instructs holding a key that can never trigger, with no grant affordance
rejected.evidence: Instruction renders `Text(t("Hold", "Зажми")) keyChip(...) Text(t("and speak..."))` but the slide receives only `dictationManager` (lines 5-9) — no permission state; without Accessibility the global hotkey monitor never fires, so the screen stays inert, the pill shows nothing (dictationManager.error never set), and Continue stays disabled.
rejected.fix: Pass `hasMicrophonePermission` and `accessibilityStatus` from OnboardingView (it already computes both) and, when not ready, replace the field with an inline card 'Dictation needs Microphone + Accessibility' + a Grant button reusing requestMicrophonePermission/openAccessibilitySettings, mirroring how OnboardingVoiceSetupSlide already takes hasMicrophonePermission.
rejected.verifyReason: Surface facts check out (line 107 renders the Hold-key instruction; the slide takes only isActive/dictationManager/onContinue with no permission state; Continue gated on hasDictatedOnce; sandbox is post-auth while the permission slide is pre-auth, so skipping is reachable), but the finding's core mechanism is contradicted by the code. (1) The hotkey DOES fire without Accessibility on this screen: GlobalHotkeyManager.start() installs LOCAL NSEvent monitors unconditionally (macos/WaiComputer/WaiComputer/Features/Dictation/GlobalHotkeyManager.swift lines 657-660 comment 'no permission required', 688, 741-757); local monitors receive events delivered to the focused app, and the sandbox auto-focuses its own field inside the frontmost WaiComputer window — only detection in OTHER apps needs the Accessibility-gated global monitor. The pipeline is live during post-auth onboarding: configure() runs at auth (WaiComputerMacApp.swift:1205) with canStart true while recording is idle, isFeatureEnabled defaults true. (2) The pill does NOT 'show nothing': startDictation (DictationManager.swift:563-582) triggers the OS microphone prompt for .notDetermined (the common skipped case — a real grant affordance), and for .denied sets dictationManager.error to localized EN/RU copy with exact System Settings instructions, which the sandbox statusPill renders (slide lines 175-183); refreshPermissionState() never clears error. (3) Missing Accessibility doesn't block completion: the slide appends the published lastFinalTranscript itself (lines 84-93, 209-236), bypassing the Accessibility-dependent paste, so hasDictatedOnce flips and Continue enables. (4) Not a dead end: footer shows 'Skip for Now' on the sandbox page (OnboardingView.swift:193). The claimed P1 'screen stays inert, hotkey can never trigger, error never set' is false; the only real residue is the narrow mic-denied-in-Settings sub-case where guidance is text-only without an inline Grant button — a minor polish item, not the described defect. The proposed fix is harmless but built on a wrong premise (it would also wrongly imply Accessibility is required to try the in-app sandbox).

confirmed.title: MacSearchViewModel is the only view model without @MainActor — search() publishes from a background thread
confirmed.evidence: `class MacSearchViewModel: ObservableObject` — every sibling VM is @MainActor (MacInboxViewModel.swift:53, MacLibraryViewModel.swift:21, …); project is SWIFT_VERSION 5.9, so the non-isolated async search() hops to the global executor and mutates @Published isLoading/results/error off-main
confirmed.fix: Annotate `@MainActor class MacSearchViewModel: ObservableObject`. One line; removes the "Publishing changes from background threads" hazard and flaky result rendering.
confirmed.verifyReason: Verified exactly as cited. MacSearchView.swift:339 reads `class MacSearchViewModel: ObservableObject` with no @MainActor, and it is the only ObservableObject view model in the macOS target without one (MacInboxViewModel.swift:53 and MacLibraryViewModel.swift:21 cite the @MainActor lines exactly; Recording:73, Import:12, ContentFeed:7, RecordingDetail:52, MacAppState, DictationManager etc. are all annotated). project.yml confirms SWIFT_VERSION "5.9" with no strict-concurrency/default-isolation flags, so under SE-0338 the nonisolated async `search(apiClient:)` (line 357) leaves the MainActor-inherited Task (MacSearchView.swift:203-205) and runs on the global executor, mutating @Published isLoading/error/hasSearched/results/unifiedResults/totalResults (lines 361-382) off-main — the exact hazard described. The fix is sensible and safe: the class is instantiated only via @StateObject in the MainActor MacSearchView (the same @MainActor+@StateObject pattern already compiles for MacLibraryViewModel/MacImportViewModel at MacContentView.swift:96-97); clear(), applySearchResponse() (DEBUG ui-test path), and query/scope bindings are all MainActor call sites; no tests or other callers exist (the iOS SearchView.swift:259 hit is a doc comment only). The annotation is language-neutral, so RU/EN behavior is unaffected.

confirmed.title: Return key fires concurrent searches with no in-flight guard — slow response overwrites the newer one
confirmed.evidence: `.onSubmit { performSearch() }` has no isLoading check (only the button has `.disabled(... || viewModel.isLoading)` at line 70), so Return during a search spawns parallel viewModel.search tasks whose responses can land out of order — results/count can end up for an older query than the field shows
confirmed.fix: Keep `private var searchTask: Task<Void, Never>?` in the VM; search() starts with `searchTask?.cancel()` and checks Task.isCancelled before assigning results, so the latest submission always wins.
confirmed.verifyReason: CONFIRMED at the cited lines in /Users/mikwiseman/Documents/Code/wai-computer/macos/WaiComputer/WaiComputer/Features/Search/MacSearchView.swift. (1) Lines 43-45: `.onSubmit { performSearch() }` on the TextField has no isLoading/in-flight guard; (2) line 70: only the Search button is `.disabled(...isEmpty || viewModel.isLoading)` — the asymmetry is exactly as described. (3) `performSearch()` (lines 195-206) guards only on empty query, then spawns an unstructured `Task { await viewModel.search(...) }` with no stored reference. (4) `MacSearchViewModel.search` (lines 357-383) has no task tracking and no Task.isCancelled checks; it reads the live `query` at task start and unconditionally writes @Published `results`/`unifiedResults`/`totalResults`/`error`/`isLoading`. So Return mid-flight (type "foo" → Return → edit to "foobar" → Return) creates parallel requests where the last network response wins: a slow older response lands after the fast newer one and leaves results/count for a query the field no longer shows. Additionally the first completion flips isLoading=false while the second request is still in flight (spinner disappears over about-to-be-replaced results). Bonus unguarded caller with the same race: the scope picker's onChangeCompat (lines 85-87) re-fires performSearch mid-flight, and since .recordings/.everything branches mutually clear each other's arrays, the landed result set can even mismatch the selected scope tab — the VM-side fix covers this caller too. FIX is sensible and safe: cancel-previous + latest-wins via `searchTask` in the VM is the standard pattern, effort S is right; no other callers exist (MacSearchViewModel is referenced only in this file), UI tests (SearchUITests) use the DEBUG applySearchResponse synchronous path and are unaffected, and the fix is pure control flow with zero copy changes so RU/EN are both unaffected. One implementation caveat: URLSession cancellation throws into the existing catch, and UserFacingErrorFormatter (shared/WaiComputerKit/Sources/WaiComputerKit/Network/UserFacingErrorFormatter.swift) has no CancellationError/URLError.cancelled special-case — so the isCancelled guard must cover the error assignment and `isLoading = false` too (guard before ANY state write), not just the results assignment, or a cancelled task will paint a bogus "Search Failed" error or hide the newer search's spinner.

confirmed.title: "No results" message quotes the live field text, not the query that was actually searched

Bonus: search() queries the trimmed string (line 358), so even at submit the quoted text can differ. Fix is sensible and safe: viewModel is private to this view, search() has one caller, and both RU/EN interpolate the same variable inline via t() so the swap is symmetric. Recommend setting lastSubmittedQuery = trimmed in search(), resetting it in clear(), and also covering the DEBUG uiTestSearchResponse path (performSearch → applySearchResponse bypasses search()), though no existing UI test asserts this copy so nothing breaks today.

confirmed.title: RU register flips between the two empty states on one screen (вы vs ты)

confirmed.title: Scope segmented control has no accessible name

p2.title: Two magnifying-glass icons sit ~30pt apart in the same search bar

p2.title: Esc does not clear the field — NSSearchField convention; the DS helper already exists

p2.fix: Add `.onEscapeKeyCompat { if !viewModel.query.isEmpty { viewModel.clear(); searchFieldFocused = true } }` to the search field, matching standard macOS search-field behavior.

p2.title: Decorative row icon is spoken by VoiceOver as the raw symbol name

confirmed.title: Overlay live preview hides the words just spoken (tail truncation of a suffix window)
confirmed.evidence: transcriptPreview returns "\"...\" + String(text.suffix(57))" (line 129) but the Text uses .lineLimit(1) + .truncationMode(.tail) — 57 chars at 11pt (~300pt) overflows the ~230pt slot, so the END (the newest speech) is clipped and the preview perpetually lags a few words behind what the user is saying.
confirmed.fix: Change .truncationMode(.tail) to .truncationMode(.head) and drop the manual "..." prefix (keep .suffix(57) as a cheap cap) so the newest words are always visible during dictation.
confirmed.verifyReason: VERIFIED REAL at the cited location. /Users/mikwiseman/Documents/Code/wai-computer/macos/WaiComputer/WaiComputer/Features/Dictation/DictationOverlayView.swift line 129 is exactly `return "..." + String(text.suffix(57))` and the Text(transcriptPreview) at line 22 has `.lineLimit(1)` (line 25) + `.truncationMode(.tail)` (line 26 — finding said 25, off by one modifier line, same spot). Width math confirmed from Core/DesignSystem.swift (Spacing.lg=16, md=12, caption=11pt, mono=13pt): panel is fixed 360pt; text column ≈ 360−32−10−~31(duration)−20(cancel)−48(4×12 spacing) ≈ 219pt (finding said ~230pt; even narrower with the hands-free badge), while "..."+57 chars at 11pt is ~330–370pt — overflow guaranteed in EN and worse in RU (wider Cyrillic glyphs). Mechanism confirmed end-to-end: DictationManager.buildTranscript() (line 1737) joins all committed segments + current interim, so interimTranscript is the full growing session transcript (>60 chars after one sentence); suffix(57) keeps the newest chars but tail truncation renders only the oldest ~30–38 of that window and clips the end, so the preview perpetually trails live speech — the newest words are never visible. The fix is sensible and safe: .truncationMode(.head) with lineLimit(1) is the supported single-line case and keeps the string end visible at any width; dropping the manual "..." avoids doubling with the system leading ellipsis; keeping .suffix(57) bounds per-update layout cost; String.suffix is grapheme-cluster-safe (RU/emoji never split). transcriptPreview is private with exactly one usage in this view and no other callers in macos/ or shared/ (grep-verified), so the change has no blast radius.

confirmed.title: Adding a duplicate dictionary word silently swallows the input
confirmed.evidence: addWord() calls "dictionaryStore.add(word: trimmed, replacement: replacement)" ignoring its Bool (store returns false on a case-insensitive duplicate, DictationDictionaryStore.swift:80) and then unconditionally clears newWord/newReplacement — the fields empty, no row appears, zero feedback; edit-mode commitWord has the same silent failure when update() returns false on collision.
confirmed.fix: Capture the Bool; on false keep the field contents and show an inline red caption under addRow: t("Already in your dictionary", "Уже есть в словаре") with a link that scrolls to / begins editing the existing entry. Apply the same to the update() == false branch.
confirmed.verifyReason: VERIFIED at the exact cited lines. DictationDictionaryView.swift:331 calls dictionaryStore.add(word:replacement:) discarding its Bool (method is @discardableResult, so silent), then lines 332-333 unconditionally clear newWord/newReplacement. DictationDictionaryStore.swift:80 returns false on a case-insensitive duplicate (word.lowercased() match) without appending — so on duplicate: fields wiped, no row, no alert/caption anywhere in the view (full file read). Bonus corroboration: the store's own learnReplacement doc comment (store lines 94-96) admits this exact "silently dropping the user's request on a name collision" failure mode but fixes it only for learned suggestions, not manual addWord; re-adding an existing word with a NEW replacement is also silently dropped. Edit-mode claim is real with one nuance: commitWord (view lines 337-344) DOES capture the Bool and skips cancelEdit() on false, so fields are kept (not swallowed) — but there is still zero feedback; Save appears dead on collision (store line 115). Fix is sensible and safe: capturing the Bool changes no API; other add() callers are the suggestion row (view line 139, untouched) and store-internal learnReplacement/update which pre-clear collisions so always succeed; t("Already in your dictionary", "Уже есть в словаре") follows the file's established t(en,ru)/OnboardingL10n pattern (correct per project i18n rule) and the RU copy is grammatical; beginEdit(_:) exists (line 350) for the edit-existing link; scroll-to needs a ScrollViewReader, consistent with effort M. Minor implementer caveat: update() also returns false for an empty word (reachable via .onSubmit on the replacement field despite the disabled button), where the duplicate message would be wrong copy — guard or branch that case.

confirmed.title: Cancelled or empty Ask Anything stream leaves the panel stuck on a spinner / "Thinking..."
confirmed.evidence: "if Task.isCancelled { return }" exits answerAskAnything without resetting isAskAnythingStreaming (and cancelDictation() at line 1324 cancels exactly this task), so the open panel shows ProgressView forever; a stream that ends with no .done and no tokens lands on the static t("Thinking...", "Думаю...") fallback (AskAnythingPanel.swift:186) with nothing running.
confirmed.fix: Wrap the body in defer { isAskAnythingStreaming = false; refreshAskAnythingPanel() }, and in AskAnythingAnswerView render an explicit dead-end state when !isAskAnythingStreaming && answer.isEmpty: t("No answer. Hold the hotkey to ask again.", "Ответа нет. Зажми клавишу и спроси ещё раз.") instead of "Thinking...".
confirmed.verifyReason: REAL, with one mechanism correction. Verified in /Users/mikwiseman/Documents/Code/wai-computer/macos/WaiComputer/WaiComputer/Features/Dictation/DictationManager.swift and AskAnythingPanel.swift. Exact citations check out: :1216 is `if Task.isCancelled { return }` and does skip the isAskAnythingStreaming reset; :1324 `stopAndInsertTask?.cancel()` cancels exactly the task running answerAskAnything (:491-493 -> stopAndInsert -> :904); AskAnythingPanel.swift:186 is the static t("Thinking...", "Думаю...") fallback. cleanup() (:1540-1583) never resets the flag or closes the panel (only hideOverlay), and the panel closes only via the X button (:1867-1870), so dead-end states persist until manually dismissed. The empty-stream half of the claim is exactly right: a stream ending with no tokens and no .done falls to :1235-1236 (flag=false, answer empty) and the open panel shows a static, lying "Thinking..." with nothing running. The cancelled half is right in outcome but wrong in mechanism: cancellation is reachable mid-stream (Esc -> GlobalHotkeyManager.swift:760-770 -> onCancelled -> cancelDictation, guard passes because state is .processing; also logout :296 and feature-off :143), but streamCompanionMessage returns AsyncStream (APIClient.swift:1669) and DictationManager is @MainActor (:46), so cancel() can only fire while the consumer is suspended in `for await`; AsyncStream then resumes nil, the loop exits normally, and :1235-1236 DO reset the flag — the cancelled case lands on the same static "Thinking..." dead-end (or the catch path with an error message if cancelled during the HTTP awaits), not an eternal ProgressView. The :1216 stuck-spinner path exists as written but is practically unreachable. So the title's user-visible defect (cancelled or empty ask leaves an open panel stuck on "Thinking..." with no retry affordance) is real; severity arguably P2 rather than P1 since the spinner variant is theoretical. The proposed fix is sensible and safe: the defer reset is idempotent, nil-safe (refreshAskAnythingPanel optional-chains :1862-1865), answerAskAnything is private with a single caller, and dictation/translation modes are untouched; the dead-end copy condition (!isAskAnythingStreaming && answer.isEmpty) cannot mask errors because every error path sets a non-empty answer (:1239-1241); RU copy "Зажми клавишу..." matches the app's established informal ты-tone (MacSettingsView.swift:1563 "Зажми \(hotkey), чтобы диктовать...", DictationCopy.swift:105 "Удерживай клавишу диктовки..."). Corrected anchor lines: dead-end fallback AskAnythingPanel.swift:184-188 plus the no-.done fallthrough DictationManager.swift:1234-1236; the cited :1216 guard is real code but a defense-in-depth target for the defer, not the live trigger.

confirmed.title: Ask Anything panel cannot be dismissed from the keyboard (no Escape)
confirmed.evidence: The borderless NSPanel ("styleMask: [.borderless, .fullSizeContentView]", canBecomeKey = true) overrides neither cancelOperation(_:) nor keyDown, and AskAnythingAnswerView has no onEscapeKeyCompat — a hotkey-summoned floating HUD that can only be closed by mousing to the 28pt X.
confirmed.fix: Override func cancelOperation(_ sender: Any?) in AskAnythingPanel to invoke a close callback wired to manager.closeAskAnythingAnswer() (the app already ships onEscapeKeyCompat for SwiftUI surfaces — DesignSystem.swift:407).
confirmed.verifyReason: VERIFIED REAL at the cited location. AskAnythingPanel.swift:5-63: borderless NSPanel (styleMask [.borderless, .fullSizeContentView] at line 12, canBecomeKey=true at line 6, init at the cited line 9) overrides neither cancelOperation(_:) nor keyDown — grep confirms zero cancelOperation overrides app-wide. AskAnythingAnswerView (same file, lines 65-194) has no onEscapeKeyCompat/onExitCommand/keyboardShortcut(.cancelAction); the only dismissal is the 28x28pt X button (lines 94-103) calling manager.closeAskAnythingAnswer(). Adversarial trace of the one potential counter-path: GlobalHotkeyManager.handleKeyDown:760 does catch Escape globally/locally and fires onCancelled, but DictationManager.swift:416-419 guards it with `state != .idle` — after the answer finishes streaming, cleanup() (DictationManager.swift:907→1540-1583) sets state to .idle while the panel stays visible, so Escape no-ops; and even mid-stream, cancelDictation()→cleanup() calls only hideOverlay() (line 1573), never closeAskAnythingAnswer() (line 1867 is the sole place the panel is hidden). No resign-key or click-outside dismissal exists; hidesOnDeactivate=false. So the panel genuinely cannot be dismissed from the keyboard. The proposed fix (override cancelOperation → close callback → manager.closeAskAnythingAnswer()) is sensible and safe: language-neutral (no RU/EN copy involved), and AskAnythingPanel has exactly one caller (DictationManager.showAskAnythingPanel(), line 1856), so no other surfaces are affected; onEscapeKeyCompat does exist at DesignSystem.swift:407 as cited. One caveat: the panel is shown via orderFrontRegardless() without makeKey, so cancelOperation alone only works after the user clicks the panel; a complete fix should also close the panel from the existing Escape branch in GlobalHotkeyManager.handleKeyDown:760-772 (or relax the .idle guard in the onCancelled wiring for the ask-anything case). Severity P1/HIG framing is fair for a hotkey-summoned floating HUD.

confirmed.title: Hands-free badge is white-on-amber — fails the contrast rule the design system codifies
confirmed.evidence: .foregroundStyle(.white) over .background(Palette.accent.opacity(0.8)) at 9pt — DesignSystem.swift:113 explicitly documents "Hardcoded white fails WCAG AA on the lighter accents (amber/green/gray ≈ 2.1–3.3:1)" and amber is the default accent; Palette.onAccent exists for exactly this.
confirmed.fix: Use .foregroundStyle(Palette.onAccent) and .background(Palette.accent) at full opacity for the hands-free capsule.
confirmed.verifyReason: Verified at the exact cited line. DictationOverlayView.swift:43 is `.foregroundStyle(.white)` on the hands-free badge (9pt semibold, line 42) over `.background(Palette.accent.opacity(0.8))` (line 46). DesignSystem.swift:113-115 contains the quoted comment verbatim ("Hardcoded white fails WCAG AA on the lighter accents (amber/green/gray ≈ 2.1–3.3:1)"), defaultAccent is .amber (=systemOrange, lines 99/134), and Palette.onAccent exists (line 263, "WCAG-safe foreground for content drawn on top of accent"). Contrast math survives adversarial scrutiny: white on amber ≈ 2.1:1, and even crediting the 0.8-opacity composite over the dark ultraThinMaterial panel it only reaches ~2.9:1 — both below the 4.5:1 AA threshold for 9pt text (no large-text exemption at 9pt). The fix is sensible and safe: it replicates the design system's own WaiPrimaryButtonStyle pattern (DesignSystem.swift:341-344, onAccent over full-opacity accent); full opacity is required by onAccent's contract (it picks black/white against the full-opacity accent — black for amber at ~9.5:1, white for blue/violet/rose); the change is color-only so RU ("Режим без рук") and EN copy from DictationCopy.handsFreeBadge are unaffected; and the badge styling has no other callers — it exists only in this view. The forced-dark overlay color scheme is not an edge case (onAccentColor resolves amber to black in both appearances).

confirmed.title: History inline edit has no cancel — Esc does nothing and accidental commits teach the dictionary
confirmed.evidence: While isEditing the only affordance is the green "checkmark.circle" commit button (plus onSubmit); there is no cancel button or Escape handler, and commitEdit → onCorrect → learningEngine.observeEdit, so the only way out of a mangled draft mutates the entry AND feeds the learned dictionary.
confirmed.fix: Add an xmark.circle cancel button next to the checkmark and .onExitCommand { isEditing = false; draft = "" } on the TextField so editing can be abandoned without committing.
confirmed.verifyReason: Verified at the cited location. Line 241 of DictationHistoryView.swift is the green checkmark.circle commit button, the only edit-mode affordance besides TextField .onSubmit (line 227) — both call commitEdit(). No cancel button, no .onExitCommand/onKeyPress(.escape)/@FocusState anywhere in the file (grep confirms the app's only Escape handling is the unused onEscapeKeyCompat helper in Core/DesignSystem.swift:407). commitEdit (lines 286-291) → onCorrect → applyCorrection (lines 180-189) → historyStore.applyCorrection + learningEngine.observeEdit with learning ON by default (?? true, line 185), so committing a mangled draft both mutates the entry and feeds the learned dictionary, exactly as claimed. Only non-affordance escapes exist (empty the draft / retype the exact original / LazyVStack row recycling). The proposed fix is sensible and safe: HistoryEntryRow is private to this file with one call site (line 72) so no other callers; the cancel button is icon-only (RU/EN-neutral, tooltip via the row's existing t() helper); draft="" is safe since draft is re-seeded on edit start (line 249). One refinement: use the existing onEscapeKeyCompat helper instead of raw .onExitCommand (onKeyPress(.escape) on macOS 14+ is more reliable on a focused TextField and is the codebase pattern).

p2.title: Dictionary editing is undiscoverable and invisible once active

p2.title: Suggestion row: two equal actions with mismatched styles, and RU "Замена" is a noun among verb buttons

p2.title: Russian stat labels don't pluralize: "1 дней подряд", "2 дней подряд"
p2.evidence: statBadge(value: "\(historyStore.streakDays)", label: t("day streak", "дней подряд")) — the RU label is fixed genitive plural, producing "1 дней подряд" and "3 дней подряд" (should be день/дня/дней).

p2.title: Overlay cancel button has no accessibility label, no tooltip, and a 20pt target

confirmed.title: Item detail header renders raw machine strings: 'url / ready / 2026-06-09T18:22:31+00:00'
confirmed.evidence: itemSubtitle = [item.source, item.status, item.occurredAt ?? item.createdAt].joined(separator: " / ") — Item.source/status are raw API enums ("url", "ready") and createdAt is an ISO-8601 String; line 243 also shows metadataLine(title: t("Created","Создано"), value: item.createdAt) raw
confirmed.fix: Parse the ISO string with ISO8601DateFormatter and render via MacDateFormatting.string(from:dateStyle:.medium,timeStyle:.short,language:) (already in DesignSystem). Map status through a t() dictionary (ready→t("Ready","Готово"), fetching→t("Fetching","Загружается"), summarizing→t("Summarizing","Обрабатывается"), failed→t("Failed","Ошибка")) and source likewise (url→t("Link","Ссылка"), paste→t("Pasted text","Вставленный текст"), telegram→Telegram). Apply the same date fix to the Created metadata line.
confirmed.verifyReason: VERIFIED REAL at the cited location. MacItemDetailView.swift lines 79-88 (the .joined(separator: " / ") is exactly at line 87) build itemSubtitle from item.source, item.status, item.occurredAt ?? item.createdAt and render it raw via Text(itemSubtitle) at line 72 in the detail header; line 243 is exactly metadataLine(title: t("Created","Создано"), value: item.createdAt). All three fields are raw String on the kit model (shared/WaiComputerKit/Sources/WaiComputerKit/Models/Item.swift:58-91); backend confirms source values are upload|url|paste|telegram|mcp:<id> (backend/app/models/item.py:73), status values are needs_input|failed|ready|fetching|summarizing (_derive_status, backend/app/api/routes/items.py:187-204), and dates are datetime.isoformat() strings (items.py:231,236) — so the header genuinely shows e.g. "url / ready / 2026-06-09T18:22:31+00:00" in both EN and RU. The fix is sensible and safe: MacDateFormatting.string(from:dateStyle:timeStyle:language:) exists at macos .../Core/DesignSystem.swift:216-254 and the neighboring MacInboxView already does this exact localized pattern for the same items (sourceLabel/statusLabel + MacDateFormatting medium/short joined with " / ", MacInboxView.swift:1284-1342); itemSubtitle and metadataLine are private so no other callers are affected. Two implementation caveats for the fixer: (1) backend isoformat() can include fractional seconds, which a default ISO8601DateFormatter fails to parse — use the two-formatter fallback precedent in shared/WaiComputerKit/Sources/WaiComputerKit/Network/APIClient.swift:115-121 (.withFractionalSeconds then plain), keeping the raw string only if both fail; (2) the proposed status map omits needs_input and the source map omits upload and mcp:<connection_id> — include those (needs_input → e.g. t("Needs input","Нужны данные"), upload → t("Upload","Файл")) or pass unknown raw values through unchanged.

confirmed.title: Failed items are dishonestly presented: list row says 'summarizing…' forever, detail says 'No summary yet.', item.error never shown
