confirmed.fix: In contentRow, branch on entry.state/status: show Text(t("failed","ошибка")).foregroundStyle(.red) for failed and keep 'summarizing…' only for fetching/summarizing. In MacItemDetailView add a "failed" case to summaryPlaceholder that shows t("Couldn't summarize this item.","Не удалось обработать материал.") plus item.error?.message in Typography.caption — mirroring the existing needsInputBanner pattern.
confirmed.verifyReason: All three sub-claims verified in code. (1) MacContentFeedView.swift:243-247 (cited line exact; `if` at 243, Text at 244): the row shows t("summarizing…","обработка…") whenever !entry.hasSummary — backend _derive_status (backend/app/api/routes/items.py:187-204) guarantees a failed item has has_summary=false and status "failed", so failed rows show "summarizing…" forever. (2) MacItemDetailView.swift:189-198: summaryPlaceholder only special-cases "fetching"/"summarizing"; "failed" hits default → "No summary yet."/"Сводки пока нет." with no spinner. (3) ItemError{code,message} exists (shared/WaiComputerKit/.../Models/Item.swift:51-54) on Item (:69) and ItemListEntry (:123) but grep across macos/ios/shared finds zero view usage — while the web already renders it (web/src/components/ItemsFeed.tsx:28-30,213: red "failed" badge + error?.message tooltip), proving data availability and Mac↔web parity gap. Fix is sensible and safe: ItemListEntry has state+status fields (prefer status — it folds metadata processing_error in); the needsInputBanner mirror pattern exists at MacItemDetailView.swift:90-104; RU+EN strings provided via the file's existing t()/OnboardingL10n pattern; summaryPlaceholder is private and its only other consumer surface (MacInboxView.swift:1853 also instantiates MacItemDetailView) benefits from the additive "failed" case without behavior change for other statuses. Minor refinement: the proposed list-row branch would leave needs_input rows with no badge (web badges it) — handle needs_input alongside failed for full honesty.

confirmed.title: Trash button deletes the item instantly — no confirmation, no undo
confirmed.evidence: Button(role: .destructive, action: onDelete) { Image(systemName: "trash") } — onDelete calls model.deleteSelected() (a hard apiClient.deleteItem) with no confirmationDialog and no undo path
confirmed.fix: Gate behind a confirmationDialog, matching the app's existing discard/Clear-All confirm pattern: @State showDeleteConfirm + .confirmationDialog(t("Delete this item?","Удалить материал?")) with a destructive t("Delete","Удалить") button. Also add .accessibilityLabel(t("Delete","Удалить")) — the icon-only button currently exposes only the English SF Symbol name to VoiceOver.
confirmed.verifyReason: VERIFIED REAL at the exact cited line. MacItemDetailView.swift:63-67 is `Button(role: .destructive, action: onDelete) { Image(systemName: "trash") }` with only `.buttonStyle(.borderless)` and `.help(t("Delete","Удалить"))` — no confirmationDialog anywhere in the file. Both callers fire the delete immediately: (1) MacContentFeedView.swift:263-265 → MacContentFeedViewModel.deleteSelected() (line 201) → apiClient.deleteItem directly; (2) MacInboxView.swift:1855-1858 → MacInboxItemDetail.deleteItem() (line 1910) → apiClient.deleteItem directly. Neither call site nor any ancestor gates it. Nuance on "hard delete": backend delete_item (backend/app/api/routes/items.py:861) is technically a soft delete (sets deleted_at), and a POST /{id}/restore route exists — but NO Swift client method or Mac UI exposes item restore (grep: zero restoreItem/item-trash hits in macos/ or WaiComputerKit), so from the user's perspective "deletes instantly, no undo" is accurate. The inconsistency claim holds: the app confirms destructive actions in 8 other places (e.g. MacRecordingDetailView.swift:534 uses exactly the proposed pattern — @State flag + .confirmationDialog with destructive t() button + cancel + message), so an unguarded item delete is a genuine P1 HIG violation. The fix is sensible and safe: gating inside MacItemDetailView covers both callers at once with no caller changes (onDelete only fires on confirm); t() is already defined in the file (line 32) and is the project-correct i18n mechanism (LanguageManager, not String(localized:)); "Удалить материал?" matches existing item copy ("Выберите материал", "Материал недоступен"). The a11y sub-claim is slightly imprecise — the SF Symbol "trash" has a system-provided VoiceOver label that is system-locale-localized (not strictly English), but it does NOT follow the in-app LanguageManager language, so adding .accessibilityLabel(t("Delete","Удалить")) is still correct and matches the file's own pattern (lines 274, 289 already use .accessibilityLabel). Effort S is right.

confirmed.title: Comparison cells have no max width — sentence-long values render as one line, exploding the table horizontally
confirmed.evidence: Text(cell(row, column.name)).font(Typography.bodySmall) sits in a Grid inside ScrollView([.horizontal, .vertical]) with no width constraint — in an unbounded horizontal context Text takes its ideal single-line width, so LLM-generated sentence values stretch each column to thousands of points
confirmed.fix: Constrain every cell and header: .frame(maxWidth: 260, alignment: .topLeading) + .fixedSize(horizontal: false, vertical: true) so values wrap to readable column widths; do the same for row.title (e.g. maxWidth: 200). Keep the horizontal ScrollView for many-column sets.
confirmed.verifyReason: REAL, line 95 exact (also headers L87, row.title L93, rationale L79). Code matches the evidence: unconstrained Text in Grid (L83) inside ScrollView([.horizontal,.vertical]) (L76). Mechanism empirically verified with ImageRenderer (same unspecified proposal a both-axes ScrollView gives content): one RU sentence cell = 855pt single line; a 4-column replica grid = 4231pt ideal width. Backend confirms sentence values are expected: comparison.py allows 8 columns, only a soft "Keep values short" prompt (no schema cap), titles capped at 200 chars. TWO CAVEATS: (1) The proposed fix is BROKEN as written — under nil×nil proposals .frame(maxWidth:260)+.fixedSize(h:false,v:true) measures 260×13 (single-line height; fixedSize is a no-op since proposals are already unspecified) while glyphs wrap to 52pt, so every Grid row collapses and wrapped text overlaps the rows below (verified numerically + rendered PNG). Use .frame(width:260, alignment:.topLeading) or .frame(idealWidth:260, maxWidth:260, alignment:.topLeading) instead — both verified wrapping cleanly at 260×52 for RU and EN. (2) Severity: MacComparisonView is currently dead code on macOS — its only caller was removed in 57d3df50a (2026-06-04) though it still compiles into the target; the identical unconstrained layout IS reachable in the shipping iOS port (ComparisonDetailView, pushed from CapturedFeedView.swift:85), which should get the same corrected fix. P1 is fair for the code defect but overstates current Mac user impact.

confirmed.title: Clicking another item keeps showing the previous item's full content until the network fetch returns
confirmed.evidence: selectItem only does `selectedItem = try await apiClient.getItem(id: id)` — between the tap (selectedId = entry.id) and the await completing, the detail pane still renders the OLD selectedItem with no loading indicator, so on slow networks the user reads item A's summary under item B's selection

confirmed.verifyReason: REAL, confirmed at the cited line. MacContentFeedViewModel.swift:141 is exactly `selectedItem = try await apiClient.getItem(id: id)` inside selectItem (139-145); nothing clears selectedItem or sets a loading flag first (`isLoading` only covers load()). The tap handler in MacContentFeedView.swift:222-225 sets `model.selectedId = entry.id` synchronously then awaits selectItem in a Task, and detailColumn (MacContentFeedView.swift:258-285) renders `if let item = model.selectedItem` with NO id match against selectedId — so after tapping item B the pane keeps rendering item A's full MacItemDetailView until the fetch returns, exactly as claimed. Minor evidence nit: the list has no visible selection highlight at all (plain List + onTapGesture, no selection binding), so "under item B's selection" slightly overstates the UI — there is zero tap feedback, which makes the issue worse, not better. The proposed view-only fix is sound and safe: Item.id and selectedId are both String so `item.id == model.selectedId` compiles; every other writer keeps the id invariant (pollUntilProcessed and pollSummaryAudioUntilReady are guarded by `selectedId == id` before assigning selectedItem; withSummaryAudio preserves id (Item.swift:93-111); setKind/deleteSelected/load-eviction nil both fields → placeholder branch). It also improves addDraft/uploadFile (spinner during the initial fetch of a just-created item). RU/EN safe: ProgressView is language-neutral and the existing localized placeholder branch is kept. No other callers affected: the VM is used only by MacContentFeedView (+ MacContentFeedViewModelTests, untouched since no VM change), and MacItemDetailView's other caller (MacInboxView.swift:1853) is outside the changed code. Two non-blocking caveats for the implementer: (1) if getItem throws, the fix shows a perpetual ProgressView (id never matches) alongside the dismissible error banner — still better than silently showing the wrong item's content, but consider an error/retry state; (2) a rapid A→B→A re-tap can let a late stale response overwrite selectedItem (pre-existing race; with the fix the symptom degrades to a spinner instead of wrong content) — an optional one-line hardening is `guard selectedId == id else { return }` after the await in selectItem, the same pattern pollUntilProcessed already uses.

rejected.title: Selected item is never highlighted in the feed list and arrow-key navigation is impossible

Mechanically the fix would otherwise type-check (selectedId is String?, ItemListEntry.id is String, no selectItem→selectedId feedback loop, RU/EN unaffected), but per the default-false-when-not-real-user-impact rule this finding is not real.

confirmed.title: Notification permission prompt fires while the app is in the background

Because the guard makes notifyTaskFinished background-only, and grep confirms this is the ONLY requestAuthorization site in the entire macOS app (configure() at app launch only sets the delegate), the first-ever notification permission prompt can structurally only appear while the user is in another app, with no context — triggered by the sole caller MacInboxView.swift:475 (onTurnCompleted, a Wai task finishing). That violates HIG guidance to request permission in context of a user action, and a contextless prompt risks .denied, which permanently silences the feature (code only logs). The proposed fix is sensible and safe: requesting on first Wai message/long-turn start while frontmost is in-context; keeping notifyTaskFinished delivery-only (.authorized/.provisional schedule, else log) affects only this one caller. Minor notes: applying .textSelection once on the container VStack in CompanionMarkdownText.body is a simpler equivalent, and since the kit is shared, the hover-reveal affordance should be adapted for iOS (always visible or context menu).

rejected.title: Unmapped tools render as raw snake_case ids with a search icon
rejected.evidence: `default: return tool` in companionToolLabel and `default: return "magnifyingglass"` (line 47) — `remember`, `forget`, `ask`, `wake_up`, `desktop_*` and any connected-MCP tool show as e.g. "remember" / "desktop_type_text" with a magnifying-glass icon implying a read/search
rejected.fix: Add map entries for the shipped tool set (remember → "Saved to your brain"/"Сохранил в память", ask → "Asked your brain"/"Вопрос к памяти", forget, wake_up, desktop_*), and for unknown tools humanize the id (strip prefix, replace underscores, capitalize) with a neutral "wrench.and.screwdriver" icon.
rejected.verifyReason: The cited code is real (CompanionTurnCards.swift:34-35 `default: return tool`, :47 `default: return "magnifyingglass"`), but the claimed P1 product impact is unreachable for every tool the finding names. tool_call events are generated ONLY from hosted mcp_call/web_search_call stream items (backend/app/core/companion.py:2944-2995), and the companion turn's hosted MCP config hard-restricts allowed_tools to ["search","fetch","list_folders","list_recordings","list_action_items"] (companion.py:1702-1708) — all mapped in companionToolLabel, as are web_search and the legacy names for old persisted rows. `ask`/`wake_up`/`forget` are public /mcp tools for external agents (OpenClaw/Hermes), never emitted to the app. `remember` surfaces as a memory_updated toast (ignored by the reducer, CompanionTurnTimeline.swift:319) and isn't even attachable in the streaming actions loop (_action_tool_defs has only send_message_telegram + desktop_open/type/click; the legacy _TOOL_DISPATCH containing remember has no callers). desktop_* tools (real names desktop_open/type/click — "desktop_type_text" doesn't exist) are approval-gated and render via CompanionActionCard, which already special-cases the desktop_ prefix with a macwindow icon and a human preview (CompanionTurnCards.swift:343); the backend comment at companion.py:2949-2950 states write hands are deliberately excluded from tool_call events. Connected-MCP tools never join companion turns (only the single "wai" server is attached). The proposed fix would add dead — and misleading — map entries ("Saved to your brain" for a path remember never takes); only the humanize-unknown-ids fallback half has merit as future-proofing, which is a hardening nit, not the asserted P1 bug.

p2.title: Failure surfaces raw error.localizedDescription twice and the typed message can't be retried
p2.evidence: `liveTurn.markInterrupted(summary: t("Failed.", "Не удалось."))` plus `errorMessage = error.localizedDescription` — the user sees "Failed." inside the assistant turn AND a red raw URLError/server string at the list bottom (line 390), with input already cleared at line 945 so retry means retyping
p2.fix: Show one surface: keep the timeline "Failed." card, replace the red raw string with a friendly offline/server message plus a "Retry" button that re-sends the optimistic message's text (restore it into `input`).

p2.title: 'Working…' placeholder branch is unreachable dead copy
p2.evidence: `Text(stage == .searching ? t("Thinking…", "Думаю…") : t("Working…", "Работаю…"))` — assistantPlaceholder only renders while `liveTurn.isEmpty`, but stage becomes .composing only on `.token` (line 1070) which makes liveTurn non-empty first, so the Working… branch never displays
p2.fix: Drop the stage ternary and always show "Thinking…" in the placeholder, or repurpose: show "Working…" when `liveTurn.hasRunningTool` (currently test-only) once tool cards exist.

p2.title: Citations from outside the passed recordings array degrade to generic 'Запись'
p2.evidence: `recordings.first(where: { $0.id == c.recordingId })?.title ?? t("Recording", "Запись")` — MacInboxView passes its currently loaded scope (MacInboxView.swift:468), so a chat citing an archived/other-folder recording shows uninformative "[1] Запись · 3:45" chips
p2.fix: Have the backend include the recording title in the citation payload (it already resolves the segment), or lazily fetch unresolved titles via apiClient and cache them in a @State [String: String].

p2.title: Starter prompts fill the composer but don't focus it or send
p2.evidence: `Button(prompt) { input = prompt }` — clicking a suggestion only sets text; the field stays unfocused and (with the missing Return-to-send) the user must click into the field and then click Send
p2.fix: Change the action to `input = prompt; inputFocused = true` — or send immediately (`Task { await send(text: prompt) }`) since these are complete, ready-to-run prompts.

p2.title: Failed approval resolutions hide the reason — detail string is dropped
p2.evidence: `case .resolved(let status, _):` discards `detail`, which CompanionView.swift:610 fills with `error.localizedDescription` on failure — the card shows only "Не удалось"/"Failed" with no cause, against the project's surface-the-error rule
p2.fix: When status == "failed" (or any non-success), render the detail string under the status row in `.font(.system(size: 12)).foregroundStyle(.secondary)` (and add it as a .help tooltip for the success/dispatched states that carry a recipient).
